description: Team-level interpretation of Y-OS execution — WHO worked, WHAT they did, WHAT value was created.
example_request: Yannick asks: 'Summarize the Y-OS operational value audit and tell me what to simplify next.'

view_1_architecture.label: Architecture View — How Y-OS processed the request
view_1_architecture.purpose: Validate that the architecture is working as designed

domains.what_happened: Request received, intent classified, rules loaded
Context pack assembled, worker assigned, provider selected
LLM called, output produced, tools invoked
Output validated, governance approved, artifact registered
Git push, Notion update, Obsidian wikilink (simulated)

view_2_team.label: Team View — Who worked on this request
view_2_team.purpose: Understand which AI team members contributed and what they produced

team_members.value_created: Defines the work to be done

team_members.output: Classified intent, selected MODE-B, assigned Ganesha worker
team_members.value_created: Routes the request to the right team member with the right context

team_members.input: Operational audit (CSO-002), Hard Core definition, CSO rules, Y-OS Constitution
team_members.output: Structured simplification recommendation: archive 22 self-referential modules, focus on 7 operational ones
team_members.value_created: Produces the actual answer — the architectural recommendation Yannick needed

team_members.output: APPROVED — all 5 Constitutional Articles passed, risk score 8/100
team_members.value_created: Ensures the recommendation is safe, constitutional, and trustworthy

team_members.output: Artifact registered with full lineage, Git push prepared
team_members.value_created: Ensures the work is never lost — searchable, traceable, reusable

team_members.output: Committed to y-os-doctrine, Notion page updated, Obsidian link created
team_members.value_created: Makes the work permanently accessible and searchable

view_3_value.label: Value View — What was actually produced
view_3_value.purpose: Understand whether Y-OS created real value or just analyzed itself

items: Measure Y-OS by task completion rate, not architecture score
Operational density confirmed: 17% (7/41 modules do real work)

value_chain.description: Yannick received a concrete, actionable recommendation in under 4 seconds for $0.044

self_referential_analysis.question: Did Y-OS produce work, or did Y-OS merely analyze itself?
self_referential_analysis.explanation: The output (simplification recommendation) is real, actionable, and addresses Yannick's question. However, the INPUT to this trace was the CSO-002 operational audit — which is itself a Y-OS self-analysis document. Y-OS used self-referential data to produce a real recommendation. The recommendation itself is not self-referential: it tells Yannick what to archive and why. This is acceptable. The risk would be if the recommendation had been 'Y-OS should build more dashboards to track itself' — it was not.
self_referential_analysis.self_referential_content_ratio: Input: 100% Y-OS data. Output: 0% self-referential (actionable recommendation).
